tweets.text:
AI regulation is far less simple than it looks. It’s prisoners dilemma at insane scale. 

In theory if all leading AI labs globally agreed to the same process of review and slow down, then we’d get frontier intelligence at similar rates and it diffuses relatively evenly. 

If the US remains at the frontier at all times, and has heavy regulation on the release of intelligence then we end up with an economic and geopolitical edge because we can control who has access to frontier intelligence. 

If we delay model releases, however, and another player - specifically China - doesn’t slow down and has equally strong models (not now but soon?) then our delays end up advantaging their models and eventually their tech stack.

Now, the US could ban these models, but that actually only puts the US at a steeper disadvantage because other countries won’t have those bans. Then, from a relative competitiveness standpoint the US has now fallen behind even though it started in front.

So, none of this is as simple as it looks. At some point it’s a simple bet of can closed models remain at the frontier in perpetuity or is there a risk of any other player or market catching up or just not falling behind.
tweets.summary_zh:
AI的监管远比看起来复杂。简单来说，如果全球顶尖的AI实验室能统一做出审查和放慢进程的决定，那大家就能平稳地获得先进的AI技术。但如果美国一直处于AI技术的前沿，却对技术发布限制重重，那么美国就可以控制谁能使用这些技术，从而保持经济和地缘政治的优势。

但如果我们拖延发布新的模型，而其他国家（尤其是中国）却不放慢速度，并且也在快速发展自己的技术，那就会使他们的技术更具优势。即使美国选择禁止这些模型，其他国家依然可以使用，这样反而会让美国处于更不利的位置，最终可能失去竞争力。

因此，AI的监管和发展不是那么简单的事。关键是封闭的模型能不能一直保持在前沿，还是有可能被其他玩家赶上。

tweets.text:
We now have de facto AI regulation. It’s not obvious why from here on out models that have certain levels of capability or are trained on certain compute sizes won’t have to be reviewed by the government before release. 

Realistically, as AI models became more and more powerful this was going to be inevitable (I think it’s too early, but here we are). So now it’s mostly just interesting to think about the implications and scenarios from here. A few would be:

* America gets to control who gets access to frontier intelligence and when. This generally works as long as we remain at the frontier at all times and don’t have a risk of being surpassed. At the moment we have a clear lead in frontier intelligence so this is a good bet, but lots of motivated parties would love to change that.

* This likely creates backlog of AI releases which means that we will see less rapid fire back and forth jumps in model progress. Bull/fine case is that we just get bigger step functions per release at a slower rate and we end up at the same point we would have. Bear case is those incremental smaller jumps were necessary for the continued flywheel of innovation. 

* Other countries likely have even more incentive to at least hedge their bets with sovereign AI strategies so aren’t dependent on access to US AI all times. Previously this was relatively moot because the alternative wasn’t good enough, but that could change out of necessity and what we’re seeing in China.

* Open weights obviously a big winner here as it becomes what likely sovereign AI gets built out on, and what (for now) can still be released to the market without the same controls. One interesting question would be how regulation eventually extends to open models, which would have its own set of long term consequences.

Anyway some big updates to everyone’s mental models of AI regulation as a result of the capabilities we’re now seeing in AI. Wild times.
tweets.summary_zh:
现在美国对AI（人工智能）有了实际的监管规则。简单来说，未来一些功能强大的AI模型在发布之前可能需要政府审核。随着AI技术越来越强大，这种情况是不可避免的，虽然我觉得现在可能还为时尚早。

从这里开始，有几个值得注意的地方：
- 美国能决定谁能获得顶尖的AI技术，这在目前我们领先的情况下是有优势的，但其他国家也在努力追赶。
- 这可能会导致AI发布的速度变慢，未来的进步可能不会像以前那么迅速。
- 其他国家为了不完全依赖美国的AI，可能会加快发展自己的AI策略。
- 开放源代码的AI模型可能因此受益，因为它们能够在监管之下发布，未来可能会有新的监管政策影响这些开放模型。

总之，随着AI技术的进步，我们在思考AI监管时需要更新思

tweets.text:
It is stupidly hard to start a pure software company right now.

Customers don't want software, they want outcomes. So you end up having to do a lot of custom work for them. 

Anything horizontal is on a ticking clock.

Every customer wants to deeply customize.

There are opportunities.

But you need to have a very heretical 2-3 year view of the world in order to build something good.
tweets.summary_zh: 现在开一家纯软件公司真是太难了。客户不只是想要软件，他们想要的是用软件实现的结果，所以你得为他们做很多定制化的工作。此外，很多基础（横向）软件的市场竞争也很激烈。每个客户都希望能深度定制产品。虽然仍然有机会，但你得有一个很独特、与众不同的长期（2-3年）视角，才能成功开发出好的产品。

tweets.text:
AI regulation is far less simple than it looks. It’s prisoners dilemma at insane scale. 

In theory if all leading AI labs globally agreed to the same process of review and slow down, then we’d get frontier intelligence at similar rates and it diffuses relatively evenly. 

If the US remains at the frontier at all times, and has heavy regulation on the release of intelligence then we end up with an economic and geopolitical edge because we can control who has access to frontier intelligence. 

If we delay model releases, however, and another player - specifically China - doesn’t slow down and has equally strong models (not now but soon?) then our delays end up advantaging their models and eventually their tech stack.

Now, the US could ban these models, but that actually only puts the US at a steeper disadvantage because other countries won’t have those bans. Then, from a relative competitiveness standpoint the US has now fallen behind even though it started in front.

So, none of this is as simple as it looks. At some point it’s a simple bet of can closed models remain at the frontier in perpetuity or is there a risk of any other player or market catching up or just not falling behind.
tweets.summary_zh:
AI的监管远比看起来复杂。简单来说，如果全球顶尖的AI实验室能统一做出审查和放慢进程的决定，那大家就能平稳地获得先进的AI技术。但如果美国一直处于AI技术的前沿，却对技术发布限制重重，那么美国就可以控制谁能使用这些技术，从而保持经济和地缘政治的优势。

但如果我们拖延发布新的模型，而其他国家（尤其是中国）却不放慢速度，并且也在快速发展自己的技术，那就会使他们的技术更具优势。即使美国选择禁止这些模型，其他国家依然可以使用，这样反而会让美国处于更不利的位置，最终可能失去竞争力。

因此，AI的监管和发展不是那么简单的事。关键是封闭的模型能不能一直保持在前沿，还是有可能被其他玩家赶上。

tweets.text:
We now have de facto AI regulation. It’s not obvious why from here on out models that have certain levels of capability or are trained on certain compute sizes won’t have to be reviewed by the government before release. 

Realistically, as AI models became more and more powerful this was going to be inevitable (I think it’s too early, but here we are). So now it’s mostly just interesting to think about the implications and scenarios from here. A few would be:

* America gets to control who gets access to frontier intelligence and when. This generally works as long as we remain at the frontier at all times and don’t have a risk of being surpassed. At the moment we have a clear lead in frontier intelligence so this is a good bet, but lots of motivated parties would love to change that.

* This likely creates backlog of AI releases which means that we will see less rapid fire back and forth jumps in model progress. Bull/fine case is that we just get bigger step functions per release at a slower rate and we end up at the same point we would have. Bear case is those incremental smaller jumps were necessary for the continued flywheel of innovation. 

* Other countries likely have even more incentive to at least hedge their bets with sovereign AI strategies so aren’t dependent on access to US AI all times. Previously this was relatively moot because the alternative wasn’t good enough, but that could change out of necessity and what we’re seeing in China.

* Open weights obviously a big winner here as it becomes what likely sovereign AI gets built out on, and what (for now) can still be released to the market without the same controls. One interesting question would be how regulation eventually extends to open models, which would have its own set of long term consequences.

Anyway some big updates to everyone’s mental models of AI regulation as a result of the capabilities we’re now seeing in AI. Wild times.
tweets.summary_zh:
现在美国对AI（人工智能）有了实际的监管规则。简单来说，未来一些功能强大的AI模型在发布之前可能需要政府审核。随着AI技术越来越强大，这种情况是不可避免的，虽然我觉得现在可能还为时尚早。

从这里开始，有几个值得注意的地方：
- 美国能决定谁能获得顶尖的AI技术，这在目前我们领先的情况下是有优势的，但其他国家也在努力追赶。
- 这可能会导致AI发布的速度变慢，未来的进步可能不会像以前那么迅速。
- 其他国家为了不完全依赖美国的AI，可能会加快发展自己的AI策略。
- 开放源代码的AI模型可能因此受益，因为它们能够在监管之下发布，未来可能会有新的监管政策影响这些开放模型。

总之，随着AI技术的进步，我们在思考AI监管时需要更新思

tweets.text:
lots of folks prepping talks next week (congrats!). Some thoughts from RLing on thousands of hours of engineer- and researcher- focused talks:

- AI generated svgs > AI generated imgs. MAXIMUM 4 ai slop images in your slides, I don't care how pretty your mom thinks they are (exception ofc if your talk is ABOUT imagegen)
- Be pointy. Better to have 1 message with 5 surprising applications, than 5 messages with no concrete examples.
- Put code on screen. Engineers like to see code. Especially if they can nitpick it to death over things that aren't the point.
- Don't forget to Entertain. Being actually funny, or good at telling relevant anecdotes, is more important than adding yet another bullet point.
- Have a Thesis. every talk gets ONE "if you remember one thing from this talk, it is this" card. 1) SO MANY PEOPLE DON'T USE IT. 2) SO MANY PEOPLE DON'T PLAN FOR IT. you get one. use it well?
- Have a Thesis Slide. you've been in that talk where everyone gets their phone out to take a photo of the slide. Because people see 1000x more images than videos, you are much more likely to have a viral talk/slide if you have a single viral slide. You are much more likely to have a viral slide if you TRY and most people do not TRY. Struggling? Collect examples from talks you like and adapt their format to your thing. Your slides have a power law - don't spend 5% of your time each on 20 slides, spend 80% of your time on 1 slide and have the rest build up to that 1 slide.
- You might not need slides. live demo in IDE, off the cuff rambles, pull audience member up to roleplay, do call and response with the audience, sing/perform, voice over vibe videos, I have seen it all. higher risk/effort, higher reward when done well.
- Be pleasant to listen to. Talks with bad/no visuals still can be listened to. Talks with bad audio are DOA. Be confident, project, remember vocal variation, evoke emotion.
- Design the emotional journey. Start strong, end strong, have a peak aha/laugh/thesis moment in the middle. Everything else is buildup.
- Data driven talks are underrated. Present pretty charts and surprising, authoritative numbers. Use the stage authority to infer from the data to support the broader thesis. Done well, it will feel like my obvious conclusion from objectively looking at the data you presented, not you feeding the conclusion to me.
- How to shill your product/company without feeling salesy: teach me everything I didn't know I should to know about the problems you solve, and then you shall have earned the right to convince me you are the guys to trust to solve it once and for all.
- Actively watch a lot of talks. like with anything you both need a lot of reps AND you need to explore to find the styles/role models that you shine in. Passive = no introspection after watching, just mindlessly autoplay next video. Active = trying to articulate why a talk was good/bad after watching. If you want to REALLY hone it, think about how you feel about a famous talk and then TRANSCRIBE the talks and read the words on the printed page, and then compare with a "normal" talk and define rules you will follow for yourself to improve.

thanks to @dexhorthy for organizing the AIEWF Speaker prep meetup tonight. we should actually do more of these....

很多人都在准备下周的演讲，恭喜他们！以下是一些演讲准备的小建议：

- 用AI生成的矢量图（svg）比用AI生成的图片要好，幻灯片里最多放4张AI图片，不管你妈妈觉得它们有多好看（如果你的演讲专门关于图像生成就另说）。
- 演讲要直截了当。用一个主题讲五个令人惊讶的应用，要比说五个没有具体例子的故事好。
- 幻灯片里放代码。工程师们喜欢看到代码，尤其是可以挑剔的地方。
- 不要忘记娱乐观众。幽默和有趣的故事比多加一条重点要重要得多。
- 有一个明确的主题。每个演讲只需要一句“如果你记住这次演讲的一件事，那就是这个。”很多人

tweets.text:
There are some subtleties in this launch that are very important in practice. This isn’t just you interacting with Claude in a 1:1 format via Slack. In this case, Claude acts as a coworker that any user can tap into in a shared way.

We’ve already seen some agentic coding systems start to adopt this pattern (as well as OpenClaw and Hermes), and doing it for general purpose knowledge work continues to push the idea forward.

As a result, what this means is that this agentic coworker needs its own set of resources, access to tools, and data to work with. This is not the same as you giving it access to your personal resources and tools, because the agent then could accidentally then share those out with anyone. 

The agent needs to instead be like any other user in the system, and you need to be thoughtful about what it should have access to, and make sure its information that is safe to share with that group.

When you can pull that off, it’s quite powerful. For instance, by connecting to Claude Tag to Box, you could have Claude access corporate sales materials for questions in sales conversations or generating RFPs, brand guidelines and marketing assets for campaign creation, product roadmap materials and product documentation for coding agents to use, contracts that anyone in the legal team can access, and more.

But this is just the Box example. You can equally have it access your product or customer analytics data, CRM information, codebase, and other resources and agents that would make sense to work on in a collective manner. 

It’s awesome to see continued innovation in what the future of work may begin to look like with agents.

tweets.text:
Are we hurtling toward a future where AI can do everything humans can?

Edwin Chen (@echen) believes we might be. He’s the CEO of Surge AI, one of the largest providers of expert data for frontier labs. Surge passed over $1 billion in revenue without raising any outside capital, and that gives Edwin a unique perspective on how quickly AI progress is accelerating.

I’m on the record arguing that AI automation actually creates more human work. I also believe that even though AI progress is accelerating exponentially, we’re much farther away from AI replacing humans than it might seem.

That’s why I had Edwin on @every’s AI & I. We batted around different visions of the future, and discussed whether humanity will retain its unique place in the universe, and what that might be.

We get into:
• If Chen’s version of the future materializes, he’s worried it’ll make people stop trying. One answer comes from a short story by science fiction writer Ted Chiang: Behave as if your decisions matter, even when you know they don’t.
• AI may soon be able to take a nebulous goal like “win a Fields Medal” and execute. What it can’t do, I argue, is set its own goals—LLMs have no intrinsic motivation, no drive to explore, no ability to just change their mind.
• A model optimized for engagement doesn’t provide the most valuable user experience. Edwin spent 20 rounds polishing a pointless email with one model before Claude told him to just send it.
• Why AI is still bad at writing: models learn to hack the metrics they're trained on. Edwin's Hemingway Bench found models outputting a metaphor in every single sentence, an overindexxing that makes for a terrible reading experience.

This is a must-watch for anyone interested in where we fit as models get more capable.

Watch below!

Timestamps
1. Introduction: 00:00:54
2. Surge as a "school for AGI": 00:01:49
3. What AI's capacity for novel mathematics says about human achievement: 00:04:46
4. Motivation in an era when AI can do everything: 00:07:29
5. The trap of optimizing AI models for engagement: 00:14:34
6. Training using datasets versus training using environments: 00:29:34
7. The value of personal data: 00:35:09
8. Why models are bad at writing: 00:39:40
9. Chen's AGI timeline: 00:42:00
tweets.summary_zh:
我们可能正在朝着一个AI能够做所有人类能做的事情的未来发展。有位名字叫Edwin Chen的CEO觉得很有可能，他的公司在前沿实验室提供专家数据，业绩相当不错。

我之前提到过，AI的自动化实际上会创造更多人类的工作机会。虽然AI进步很快，但我觉得它完全取代人类还需要很长时间。

这次我和Edwin聊了不同的未来想法，讨论了人类是否还能保持独特的位置。他提到，如果AI真的能做所有事情，可能会让人们失去努力的动力，同时我们也探讨了AI的目标设定能力和写作质量的问题。

如果你对AI的未来和人类的关系感兴趣，这个讨论一定值得一看！

tweets.text:
This is a new paradigm for interacting with Claude that is significantly more "inline" with all the other human activity org-wide. Once you do all of the under the hood engineering work to make this "just work" (e.g. across tools, integrations, compute environments, memory, security, etc.), Claude basically joins the team in a seamless way - you can talk to it as you would talk to a person and it can help with a very large variety of workloads.

Imo this is the 3rd major redesign of LLM UIUX. The first paradigm was that the LLM is a website you go to, the second was that it is an app you download to your computer. This third one is that it is a self-contained, persistent, asynchronous entity with org-wide tools and context, working alongside teams of humans. It really takes a while to wrap your head around it, but it works and it is awesome.

tweets.text:
Good post on the dynamics of AI pricing right now. We’re basically going to see a barbell dynamic on pricing between high cost frontier models and cheap but good open or closed weights models. 

Then the question is how do you maximize efficiency from the frontier models, and get better quality and performance of the cheaper models. That’s where the applied AI layer comes in.

The applied layer can route to the best model at any time given the workload at hand, as well as mitigate either high token costs or worse performance. The closer you are to the underlying workflow, the better you can tune the use of models for a given business process. 

That’s all about understanding the workflows, domain specific FDEs, evals by customer or process, getting data setup well for agents, and more. Overall good for applied AI.
tweets.summary_zh: 现在，AI 的定价情况变得很有趣。有些模型价格高，但性能顶尖；而另一些则便宜但也不错。关键是怎么最大化利用这些高端模型，同时提高那些便宜模型的质量。这就需要一个应用层来帮助选择最佳模型、降低高成本或性能不佳的问题。整体来说，这对应用型 AI 是个好消息。

tweets.text:
Almost all AI model and agent progress is downstream from evals. Open weights post training for specific domains comes down to evals. Agent improvements in the applied AI layer is all about evals. Agentic enterprise deployments that actually can augment work is all about evals. It’s all evals.

This will become a core competency of any enterprise in the future. The companies that are able to best understand their own (and/or customers) workflows and how well agents participate in that work will be in the best position to actually drive real automation.
tweets.summary_zh: 几乎所有的 AI 模型和智能体的进步都跟评估（即测试和衡量效果）有关。未来，企业能否理解自己的工作流程，并知道 AI 如何参与其中，将决定它们能否实现真正的自动化。这意味着“评估”将成为每个企业的一项重要能力。

tweets.text:
Another new idea to push the state of AI architectures forward. Sakana released a model that effectively uses a mixture of models to get work done. You get a single API but then the work gets farmed out the model that best performs the task. 

“Fugu manages model selection, delegation, verification, and synthesis automatically. It solves tasks directly when that is enough, or coordinates a team of expert models when a problem calls for more. The complexity of a multi-agent system never reaches your code.”

This is generally how applied AI products are building their agent harnesses at this point, but the idea of making this an LLM that any developer can interact with is also a great idea. As we get more innovation with both frontier closed and OSS models, there’s going to be a ton of value produced for the layer that can route the best.
tweets.summary_zh:
又有一个新概念可以推动人工智能的发展。Sakana发布了一个新模型，它能聪明地选择最佳的子模型来完成任务。你只需使用一个接口（API），但实际工作会分配给最能胜任的模型。

这个新模型能自动管理不同任务的分配和执行，解决问题时可以直接处理，也可以协调多个专业模型合作。这样，即使是复杂的后台系统，开发者也不用太操心。

现在的AI产品在构建智能代理时普遍用着这样的方式，而如果能让更多开发者方便地使用这个大型语言模型（LLM），那也是个不错的主意。随着更多创新的出现，能有效选择最佳方案的系统会带来很多价值。

tweets.text:
Agents will use software 100X more than people. 

When that happens, theres a huge need for guardrails on what the agents are doing so they don’t leak data or change the wrong information, authoritative sources of truth for them to work with, logging and auditing of what they’re doing, the ability to collaborate with people through these systems, and more.

A simple query on any given agentic task could pull in more data than a user touches in a month. As a result, there are lots of categories of software that when it goes headless that the usage and value go up substantially. Agents will end up using our CRM data, documents and corporate knowledge, analytics data, and other information far more than people ever did.

The platforms that can move toward the model of powering these headless interactions, and have a business model and technology strategy to support this, will be in the best position in the future.
tweets.summary_zh: 未来，软件代理（自动执行任务的程序）会比人类使用软件多得多。为了确保这些代理正确工作，避免泄露数据或错误修改信息，我们需要为它们设置规则、提供权威的信息来源，以及记录和审计它们的操作。简单来说，这意味着代理将比我们人类更频繁地利用公司资源和数据，能够更高效地完成任务。那些能适应这种新趋势、提供支持的企业，将会在未来占据有利位置。

tweets.text:
A good rule of thumb for preventing AI slop (in writing, design, etc):

Is your input (the context) longer than the output? 

I've found that for the AI to produce quality results, my input is often 3-5 times the length of the output.

If your input is much shorter than the output, it's almost certainly going to produce slop
tweets.summary_zh: 为了避免 AI 生成的内容质量差（比如写作、设计等），可以参考一个简单的原则：你的输入信息（背景）要比输出内容长。经验告诉我，好的输入通常是输出的 3 到 5 倍。如果输入太短，输出结果肯定很糟。

builders.bio: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.

tweets.text:
The Product role is having an identity crisis too.

Engineering has found its AI-native interface - SWE agents dramatically increase individual leverage.
Companies are asking PMs to use AI, but they haven't evolved the role.

So there are two camps.

The old-school PM.
AI accelerates the old job: more PRDs, more strategy decks, more docs. Lots of output, not much judgment.

The Builder PM.
Builder PMs use AI to expand their role across the product lifecycle.

They explore a much larger surface area of ideas to arrive at the best. They run agents for market and user research, query logs and analytics directly, generate competing ideas, then curate the best ones.

They constantly evolve their workflows to take advantage of the latest in AI.

Their outputs are increasingly prototypes rather than docs - engineers react far more constructively to demos than docs.

They do this all without compromising on the craft -they still form a strong point of view on what should be built and why.

I think the role is moving much closer to Builder PMs.
tweets.summary_zh: 产品经理（PM）的角色现在面临一些变化。传统的PM还停留在过去，只是增加工作量，比如写更多文档；而新型的“Builder PM”则利用AI来拓宽自己的职责，从市场调研到生成创意，做出更有价值的原型。总体来看，产品经理的工作越来越接近于“Builder PM”的方式。

tweets.text:
Pretty remarkable what’s happening with open weights AI right now. We’re seeing models achieve SOTA results on specific tasks, and getting close to frontier on some areas of coding and other domains. 

The more that open weights is able to maintain only a marginal gap from the frontier, instead of a widening gap, the more value that can be created with AI.

Incidentally, this is actually fine for the frontier labs as well; if we can lower the cost of an overall task then AI usage goes up in general. You’re still likely using frontier models for planning, orchestration, reviewing, and other parts of work. 

But this is all very good for the applied layer of AI, which is now in a great position to cost optimize workloads with cheaper models or use tailored open models post-trained for specific tasks to improve performance.
tweets.summary_zh:
开放权重的人工智能目前正在取得很大进展，我们看到一些模型在特定任务上表现非常优秀，甚至在编码等领域也接近最前沿。如果开放权重能够保持与最前沿技术的差距较小，那么 AI 创建的价值就能更多。

这对最前沿的实验室也没什么坏处，因为如果整体任务的成本降低，AI 的使用会更多。你可能还是会用到最先进的模型进行计划、协调、审核等工作。

总之，现在应用层的 AI 能够利用便宜的模型来优化工作负担，或者根据特定任务调整后的开放模型来提升性能，这是一件好事。

tweets.text:
The biggest problem with AI is that priors need to be reset every few weeks.. and it seems like most people are incapable of doing that. 

I talk to so many people who say xyz doesn’t work and when I ask when was the last time they tried testing it, the answer is always “a few months ago.” 

Brother that’s like eons ago in AI timelines. This is why each person needs to have their own evals of hard tasks and weekly tinker time to truly understand where the frontier is. 

And then you need to talk to enterprise buyers weekly who are usually two years behind. But they are the buyers so you need to understand how to market. 

The combination of the two will give you a pretty good sense of where to invest time and capital. If you just do these two weekly, you’ll be ahead of 99% of people.
tweets.summary_zh:
AI 的最大问题是，旧的知识和经验每隔几周就得更新一次，但很多人做不到。这就像他们几个月前用过的技术，这对现在来说根本不够新。 

所以，每个人都需要定期评估自己的工作，了解最新情况，并每周与企业客户沟通，因为他们往往滞后两年。只要做到这两点，你就能比99%的人走得更远。

Codex has won me over because:

Personal account. All opinions come from my training data.

tweets.text: I had chronic pain for most of my life until a doctor did an MRI of the pain source and found a congenital condition that was then fixed with surgery. Now I'm wondering if I had 30+ years of pain because doctors worried I was too stupid to be in the presence of scan results.
tweets.summary_zh: 有人分享了自己多年忍受慢性疼痛的经历，直到医生做了MRI（核磁共振成像，检查身体内部状况）才发现是先天性疾病，经过手术治疗才好了。她好奇是不是因为医生觉得自己不够聪明，所以没给她看检查结果，才让她忍受了这么多年的痛苦。

tweets.text: A counter to this is "yes but people *don't* ignore it". But not ignoring things on scans is our norm because, until recently, we only did scans if there was a clear need. If we move to a scan-more-often paradigm, the norms of what we do with that information will surely adjust.
tweets.summary_zh: 有些人说“是的，但人们并不会忽视这些信息”，但其实我们习惯上并不会忽视扫描结果，因为之前我们只在确实有需要的时候才进行扫描。如果我们现在更频繁地进行扫描，处理这些信息的方式肯定会改变。

tweets.text: The view that we shouldn't do more medical scans because incidental findings cause a lot of harm doesn't sit well with me. It seems like the issue it points to isn't the scan but the response to it. If you see something on a scan but have no other symptoms, you could ignore it.
tweets.summary_zh: 有些人觉得不应该多做医学扫描，原因是扫描后发现的意外问题可能会造成很大困扰，但我并不这么认为。其实问题不在于扫描本身，而是我们对扫描结果的反应。如果扫描发现了什么但没有其他症状，可以选择不在意。

The next hot programming language is… markdown.

A minimal eve agent:

📂 𝚊𝚐𝚎𝚗𝚝/
  📄 𝚒𝚗𝚜𝚝𝚛𝚞𝚌𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗𝚜.𝚖𝚍

tweets.text:
The main variable in getting success with agents is whether you can get the agent the context it needs to do its work; and a major factor in that is if you can create a shared working area for that agent that a human can understand as well. 

This is one of the reasons why agents using file systems is such a big deal. It creates a unified system that both the person and the agent can work within to pass around data. 

“What they need is a working set: plans, notes, task lists, policies, drafts, summaries, logs, corrections,  decisions, etc. For that layer, a filesystem-shaped interface tends to be more legible to both the model and the humans supervising it.”

It turns out giving agents access to the systems we already know how to use, but in a way that is best optimized for them, is the perfect primitive for agents to work.
tweets.summary_zh:
要让 AI 助手（代理）成功工作，关键在于能否给它提供必要的信息和一个人类也能理解的工作环境。使用文件系统是个很重要的原因，因为它可以让人和 AI 在同一个平台上方便地分享和处理数据。

简单来说，给 AI 助手一个我们熟悉的工作空间，让它更容易理解我们的需求，能帮它更好地完成任务。

tweets.text:
It took us a lot of iterations to find the right pattern for this. Early on we were very naive and we thought that the agent could one shot project updates without much input from users.

That approach ended up creating a bunch of slop, because users disengaged their brain from the process of writing an update.

By switching to a multi turn interactive mode, the agent prompts you and asks you what to emphasize, what's most important, what context is missing, etc.

This simple change elicits much better project updates and ensures that the user has a chance to steer rather than simply being asked to say yes to everything.

A very good example of introducing friction for a net benefit.
tweets.summary_zh:
经过多次尝试，我们发现了正确的方法。一开始我们想当然地以为自动更新项目的助手可以一次性搞定，但这样让用户反而不太参与，更新内容乱七八糟。

后来我们改成了互动模式，助手会询问你哪些内容最重要、缺少什么信息等等。这个简单的改变让项目更新变得更好，也让用户能够更积极地参与，而不是只是一味点头。

这是个很好的例子，说明适当增加一些麻烦其实是有益的。

tweets.text:
The fact that open weights models are being discussed credibly at this level of capability should be a huge update for many. 

The implications of open models getting to frontier performance ensures that you can always have sovereign AI, have the ability to post train for your specific workflows, cost optimize for various workloads, and actually afford to do much more with AI (which opens up meaningfully different applications).

Huge win for the applied AI layer.
tweets.summary_zh: 开放权重模型（open weights models）现在被认真讨论，表明技术已经达到了新的水平。这意味着我们可以有更自主的人工智能，可以根据自己的需求进行优化，并且能用得起更先进的应用。对使用人工智能的领域来说，这是个大好消息！

tweets.text:
This is a good update for getting access to Fable. It also gives us a view into what the future is likely going to look like with AI regulation. 

The government will have frameworks that are used to determine future model releases past a certain threshold of capability or compute levels. Given all the constituents involved, and the economic and societal significance of AI, this was practically an inevitability.

It may seem small but the implications are massive. It will mean that each model update will go through an extensive review, testing, and feedback process. And in that processes lots of groups will weigh in on the risk of the model, and there will be lots of subjectivity on what the actual risks are or practicalities of exploiting those risks. 

A positive potential future here would be we still get massive model progress but they just happen in bigger jumps at once, where the labs pack in major improvements since the cost and slow down of each review stacks up. 

On the other side, the risk is that past a certain threshold we may not get to see the rapid back and forth of model progress that we’ve gotten used to which can have negative compounding effects. Hoping for the former outcome.
tweets.summary_zh:
这个更新让我们可以更好地接触到 Fable，同时也给我们展示了未来 AI 监管的情景。政府会制定一些框架，来评估未来 AI 模型的发布，特别是在它们能力或运算水平超过某个界限时。虽然这个听起来小，但其实影响很大！

每次模型更新都需要经过严格的审查、测试和反馈，这个过程会涉及很多团体讨论模型可能带来的风险。希望未来的进展虽然慢一点，但能做到一次性大幅提升，而不是看到的快速反复更新，这样可能会有负面的连锁反应。总的来说，我希望能看到积极的结果。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — GLM 5.2 的创新特性与创意设计和电商运营有直接关系，可以为 Logic Labs 和 TexTale 的产品开发提供灵感。

podcasts.name: Y Combinator

这个视频讲述了如何在创业初期获得第一批客户，分享了多种实用策略和技巧，包括有效的市场调研、建立人脉和运用社交媒体等。通过实际案例，讲者深入探讨了初创企业在客户获取过程中常见的误区和有效的方法。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个视频提供了创业过程中获取客户的实用策略，能够帮助 Logic Labs 和 TexTale 在市场推广和客户获取方面更具竞争力。

podcasts.name: No Priors
podcasts.title: Intel CEO: America Has No Choice
podcasts.summary_zh:
这段视频中，英特尔首席执行官谈论了美国在全球科技竞争中的地位，强调了必须加大对半导体产业的投资以保持竞争力。他指出，面对日益激烈的国际竞争，美国必须做出正确的选择，以确保未来的科技发展和经济增长。

💡 值得一看 — 了解全球半导体产业的发展趋势和美国在这一领域的挑战，对于逻辑实验室和创意设计方向的应用具有启发性。

podcasts.name: Data Driven NYC
podcasts.title: The Real Bottleneck in the AI Boom #ai #podcast
podcasts.summary_zh:
这段播客讨论了如今 AI 发展的真正瓶颈，强调了技术的普遍可用性与实际应用中的障碍，尤其是人才和基础架构方面的挑战。节目中还讨论了如何克服这些问题，加速 AI 创新和落地。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 作为 Logic Labs 的联合创始人，这段对 AI 发展瓶颈的深度解析将帮助你更好地理解行业现状，以便在创意设计和电商运营中把握未来趋势。

podcasts.name: Peter Yang
podcasts.title: Why HeyGen built their AI video agent on HTML
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频讨论了 HeyGen 为什么选择在 HTML 上构建他们的 AI 视频代理。视频深入探讨了这一选择的技术背景以及其在功能性和可扩展性上的优势，展示了如何利用简单而强大的工具来实现复杂的 AI 功能。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个视频可以帮助您理解将 AI 与网页技术结合的思路，对 Logic Labs 的技术开发和 TexTale 的电商策略都具有启发性。

podcasts.name: Latent Space
podcasts.title: ⚡️Every product of the future will be a living system  — Ronak Malde, Trajectory.ai
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频探讨了未来产品将如何变成“活系统”，即它们能够自我学习和适应用户需求。Ronak Malde 分享了对这一趋势的见解，并阐述AI在产品设计和用户体验中的重要作用。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个话题与 Logic Labs 在 AI 方面的创新密切相关，也能为 TexTale 提供关于未来科技服装的创意灵感。

podcasts.name: Data Driven NYC
podcasts.title: The Expanding Cone of AI Demand #ai #podcast
podcasts.summary_zh:
本期视频探讨了人工智能需求的快速增长趋势，分析了各个行业如何逐步采纳 AI 技术并适应新环境。主持人邀请了几位行业专家，一起深入讨论未来的发展方向及潜在的商业机会。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 本期内容涉及 AI 需求的提升与行业应用，能够为 Logic Labs 和 TexTale 的战略规划提供有价值的见解。

podcasts.name: Peter Yang

这个视频由Bin Liu和Jake Moran主讲，介绍了如何使用Hyperframes这一免费工具制作专业的视频发布内容。视频详细讲解了每个阶段的操作，让观众能够轻松上手，无需专业知识就能创作出吸引人的视频。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个教程可以帮助你在TexTale中使用AI制作高质量的营销视频，提升品牌传播效果。

podcasts.name: Lenny's Podcast

在这期播客中，Fiona Fung分享了构建世界上最优秀的AI工程团队的经验，并探讨了如何通过跨学科合作提升团队的创造力和效率。她还谈到了在快速变化的AI行业中，团队成员应具备的技能与思维方式。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这期播客对AI工具的开发和团队建设提供了宝贵见解，适合Logic Labs和TexTale在推动创意设计和技术结合时的参考。

podcasts.name: The AI Daily Brief
podcasts.title: The 5-Minute AI Weekly Recap: Realignment Week
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频总结了过去一周 AI 行业的重要动态，主要聚焦于各大公司在人工智能产品和策略上的调整与重新定位。它探讨了这些变化对市场的潜在影响，并梳理了当前 AI 领域的一些新趋势。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 该视频提供的行业洞察和趋势分析，可以帮助 Logic Labs 和 TexTale 在产品开发和品牌营销中做出更精准的决策。

podcasts.name: No Priors
podcasts.title: Intel CEO on Elon Musk
podcasts.summary_zh:
视频中，Intel CEO 讨论了对 Elon Musk 及其在科技和商业影响力方面的看法。他分享了与 Musk 在创新、领导力以及科技行业未来发展方向的不同见解，特别是 AI 如何在这些变革中扮演重要角色。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 该视频能够帮助你理解行业领军人物对 AI 未来的思考，对 Logic Labs 和 TexTale 在创意设计与品牌营销方面的策略形成启发。

podcasts.name: Data Driven NYC
podcasts.title: The Misleading Data on GPU Prices #ai #podcast
podcasts.summary_zh:
该视频讨论了GPU价格的误导性数据，揭示了当前市场上关于显卡价格波动的真实情况。通过数据分析，节目主持人指出了一些常见的误区，并解释了这些数据如何影响AI行业和科技产品的开发。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 本视频分析了GPU价格的动态，直接关系到Logic Labs在AI工具产品开发中的成本控制和市场策略，同时也对TexTale在电商运营中的技术选择可能有所帮助。

podcasts.name: Peter Yang
podcasts.title: The 3 AI apps I keep coming back to

这个视频介绍了三款令人印象深刻的人工智能应用，这些应用能够提升生产力和创意，通过实际案例向观众展示如何在日常工作中有效利用它们。每个应用都有其独特的功能，以帮助用户更好地实现创意和任务管理。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这些应用工具可以直接提升您在 Logic Labs 和 TexTale 的工作效率，特别是在创意设计和电商运营中。

podcasts.name: The AI Daily Brief
podcasts.title: Your Company Doesn't Need an AI Strategy
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频探讨了企业在AI应用上普遍存在的误解，强调许多公司并不需要制定复杂的AI战略，而是应该专注于实际的业务问题和目标。通过实例说明，成功实施AI的关键在于识别如何有效地利用技术来提高工作效率和成效。

💡 值得一看 — 虽然视频不专注于具体的AI工具，但对于如何将AI融入现有业务中给出了重要的洞察，这对于Logic Labs和TexTale的运营与创新都具有启发性。

podcasts.name: Tiago Forte
podcasts.title: Get perfect AI answers every time 🧠 #ai #claudeai #chatgpt
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频讨论了如何通过有效的问题设计，获得 AI（如 Claude 和 ChatGPT）提供的最佳回答。Tiago Forte 分享了具体的策略和技巧，帮助观众在与 AI 的互动中更好地获取所需的信息。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 该视频提供了优化 AI 交互的实用建议，可以帮助 Logic Labs 和 TexTale 在产品开发和电商运营中提升效率与创意。

podcasts.name: Peter Yang
podcasts.title: Just Launch What You Build, Even If No One Cares
podcasts.summary_zh:
在这个视频中，Peter Yang 讨论了创业过程中一个常见但重要的看法：不要因为没有外界的关注而犹豫，勇敢推出你所开发的产品。视频强调了实践的重要性，并鼓励创业者关注自己的创意而不是寻求他人的认可。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个视频的观点与 Logic Labs 和 TexTale 的创业过程密切相关，适合激励他在创意设计和产品开发中更加大胆。

podcasts.name: The AI Daily Brief
podcasts.title: The Models Trying to Replace Fable
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频探讨了几个新的模型，它们试图取代原本被广泛使用的Fable工具，聚焦于如何利用AI技术提升创意设计和产品开发的效率。内容涵盖了AI在创作过程中的应用，以及这些替代品的优缺点。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个视频与Logic Labs在AI产品开发中的应用相关，同时也能为TexTale的创意设计提供启示。

podcasts.name: Fireship
podcasts.title: The most trusted code on Earth is being rewritten in Rust
podcasts.summary_zh:
这个视频讨论了Rust语言在编程界的重要性，强调了它在重写信任度最高的代码中的作用。Rust因其内存安全和并发处理能力，正逐渐成为高性能和安全性需求高的项目的新宠。

💡 值得一看 — 该视频为了解Rust在当前科技界的趋势提供了深刻的见解，可能对Logic Labs和TexTale在技术选择和产品开发方面有所启发。

podcasts.name: Data Driven NYC
podcasts.title: Why Cloud Compute is Not a Commodity #ai #podcast
podcasts.summary_zh:
这期视频讨论了云计算是否真的可以被视为商品，分析了云计算的定价模式、市场竞争以及对技术企业的影响。主持人深入探讨了云计算的价值，以及它与传统商品之间的区别，让观众能够更好地理解这个领域的复杂性和潜在机遇。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个话题与 Logic Labs 的 AI 工具开发密切相关，帮助你更好地理解云计算在 AI 解决方案中的作用与价值。

podcasts.name: Y Combinator
podcasts.title: The Age Of The 40-Year-Old Solo Founder Is Here
podcasts.summary_zh:
这段视频讨论了一个趋势：越来越多的创业者选择独自创业，尤其是40岁左右的创始人。视频中讲述了他们的独特优势、市场机会以及如何利用现代技术（包括 AI）来实现自己的商业梦想。

🎯 推荐 Yves 观看 — 这个趋势与 Logic Labs 的创新背景及 TexTale 的电商模式息息相关，能够为你在创意设计和品牌营销过程中提供新的思路。

producthunt.name: Fundraisly
producthunt.tagline: AI fundraising agent that finds investors and books meetings
producthunt.summary_zh: Fundraisly 是一个智能募资助手，利用人工智能技术帮助用户寻找投资者并预约会议。它可以简化募资流程，让用户更高效地与潜在投资者沟通。

producthunt.name: Upstream
producthunt.tagline: The inbox designed for humans and agents
producthunt.summary_zh: Upstream是一款专为用户和客服人员设计的收件箱工具。它通过智能整理消息，让信息更清晰，帮助用户和代理快速找到重要内容，提升沟通效率。

producthunt.name: Goldfish
producthunt.tagline: Press Option. It knows your work and replies like you
producthunt.summary_zh: Goldfish是一款智能助手工具，通过按下选项按钮，它可以根据你的工作习惯和风格，自动生成回复信息。无论是邮件、消息还是文档，它都能像你一样进行智能回应，提升你的工作效率。

producthunt.name: Bond
producthunt.tagline: The AI to-do list that does itself
producthunt.summary_zh: Bond是一款智能待办事项应用，能够自动管理和完成任务。用户只需输入任务，Bond会利用人工智能技术安排和执行，省去手动操作的时间和精力。

producthunt.name: Mailwarm 2.0
producthunt.tagline: The email warmup tool, upgraded for deliverability.
producthunt.summary_zh: Mailwarm 2.0 是一款邮箱加热工具，旨在提高电子邮件的投递率。它通过模拟真实用户的互动，帮助提升新邮箱的信誉，确保邮件能够顺利送达收件人。该工具简化了邮件营销的准备工作，增强了发件箱的表现。

producthunt.name: Publora
producthunt.tagline: A publishing API for agents to post on 10 social platforms
producthunt.summary_zh:
产品名：Publora  
Publora是一个发布API，允许用户将内容同时发布到10个社交平台。通过这个工具，用户可以轻松管理多个社交媒体账号，并提高内容发布的效率。

producthunt.name: Bluerails Discovery
producthunt.tagline: The rails AI agents use to find and pay you
producthunt.summary_zh: Bluerails Discovery 是一款智能代理工具，能够自动分析和发现合适的机会来为用户赚取收入。它利用人工智能技术，帮助用户快速找到并支付相关资源，简化了收入获取的过程。

producthunt.name: Pancake
producthunt.tagline: OpenClaw in Slack that makes your company autonomous
producthunt.summary_zh: Pancake是一个集成在Slack中的工具，旨在提高公司内部的自主性。它通过自动化管理任务和流程，帮助团队更高效地协作，减少依赖人工干预。用户可以轻松设置和跟踪工作进展。

producthunt.name: Elentaria
producthunt.tagline: Your GTM: from diagnosis to execution
producthunt.summary_zh: Elentaria是一款市场营销工具，旨在帮助企业从市场诊断到执行战略。它提供数据分析和战略规划，使用户能够更高效地制定并实施市场进入策略。

producthunt.name: SellerClaw
producthunt.tagline: A team of AI agents that runs your stores across channels
producthunt.summary_zh: SellerClaw是一款智能商店管理工具，由一组AI代理组成。它可以在多个销售渠道上自动运营和管理你的商店，处理订单、库存和客户沟通，提高效率。用户只需最小的干预即可实现全面的商店管理。

producthunt.name: Tencent EdgeOne Makers
producthunt.tagline: Ship AI agents like web apps, in minutes.
producthunt.summary_zh: Tencent EdgeOne Makers 是一款工具，允许用户快速创建和部署 AI 代理，就像使用网页应用一样简单。用户可以在几分钟内开发出具备智能交互功能的应用程序，提升工作效率。该产品支持多种场景，适合不同的应用需求。

producthunt.name: Honen
producthunt.tagline: Automated teaching + learning infrastructure for any company
producthunt.summary_zh: Honen是一种自动化的教学与学习系统，适用于各类公司。它通过智能化的方式帮助企业提供高效的培训和知识共享，让员工更轻松地学习和提升技能。利用这一平台，公司可以更好地管理和优化内部培训流程。

producthunt.name: InsForge Backend Branching
producthunt.tagline: Git style branching for your backend
producthunt.summary_zh: InsForge Backend Branching 是一款用于后端开发的工具，采用类似Git的分支管理方式。它让开发者可以轻松创建、切换和管理不同的代码版本，便于团队协作和代码整合。通过这种方式，开发者可以在不影响主代码的情况下进行实验和迭代。

producthunt.name: Honestly
producthunt.tagline: See what Reddit and TikTok honestly think about your product
producthunt.summary_zh: Honestly 是一款工具，可以让你了解 Reddit 和 TikTok 上用户对你产品的真实看法。通过分析平台上的评论和反馈，帮助你获取宝贵的市场洞察和用户体验信息。

producthunt.name: AgentX
producthunt.tagline: Evaluate AI agent, pinpoint issues, and fix with one click.
producthunt.summary_zh: AgentX 是一款智能工具，可以快速评估 AI 代理的表现，自动识别问题并通过一键操作进行修复。用户可以轻松管理和优化 AI 系统，提高工作效率。

github_trending.description: SimpleX - the first messaging network operating without user identifiers of any kind - 100% private by design! iOS, Android and desktop apps 📱!
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个即时通讯网络，设计上完全不使用用户身份信息，因此能够保护用户隐私。对于Yves来说，这个项目可以为Logic Labs提供一个隐私保护的通讯工具，帮助团队在创意设计和技术开发中安全地交流，同时也可以提升TexTale在处理客户信息时的隐私保护能力，增强品牌形象。

github_trending.description: A format specification for describing a visual identity to coding agents. DESIGN.md gives agents a persistent, structured understanding of a design system.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目旨在为编码代理提供一个格式化规格，帮助它们持久并结构化地理解设计系统，从而在开发过程中更好地执行设计理念。对于Yves来说，这个项目对TexTale（科技服装电商）有很大的帮助，因为它可以帮助团队更好地将品牌视觉识别应用到产品中，同时提高开发效率。此外，作为创意设计从业者，Yves也能从中获取灵感，提升他在品牌营销方面的创新能力。

github_trending.description: openpilot is an operating system for robotics. Currently, it upgrades the driver assistance system on 300+ supported cars.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个用于改进汽车驾驶辅助系统的开源软件，它可以让超过300款支持的汽车拥有更智能的驾驶体验。对Yves来说，这个项目的技术可以为Logic Labs提供在自动驾驶和AI领域的创新灵感，同时也能为TexTale的科技服装项目开辟智能穿戴设备设计的新方向，提升用户体验。作为创意设计从业者，openpilot展示了如何通过技术提高日常生活中的便捷性，为他在创意设计上带来更多想象空间。
这个项目提供了一个Git工具，可以在进行代码上传时，防止常见的错误，比如意外提交不必要的文件或代码。对于Yves来说，这个工具可能对Logic Labs（AI 公司）有帮助，因为在AI项目的开发中，确保代码的整洁和准确性是至关重要的，同时也有助于提升团队的工作效率。

github_trending.description: The open and composable observability and data visualization platform. Visualize metrics, logs, and traces from multiple sources like Prometheus, Loki, Elasticsearch, InfluxDB, Postgres and many more.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个用于监测和可视化数据的平台，可以帮助用户从多个数据源获取并展示指标、日志和跟踪信息。对于Yves来说，这个工具可以在Logic Labs的AI项目中用于实时监控和分析数据表现，同时也可以为TexTale提供关于电商运营效果的深入洞察，帮助提升品牌营销策略。作为创意设计从业者，Grafana可以为他提供可视化的数据支持，从而更好地策划和优化设计方案。

github_trending.description: A list of SaaS, PaaS and IaaS offerings that have free tiers of interest to devops and infradev
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个汇总列表，列出了许多有免费使用额度的云服务，主要供开发运维人员使用。对Yves来说，这个项目对Logic Labs（AI 公司）尤其有帮助，因为它可以帮助团队在开发和部署AI相关项目时，找到合适的云服务，从而节省成本并提高效率。同时，作为创意设计从业者，他也可以使用这些工具来迅速实现创意和实验。

github_trending.description: Transforms complex documents like PDFs and Office docs into LLM-ready markdown/JSON for your Agentic workflows.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目的主要功能是将复杂的文件（如PDF和Office文档）转换为易于处理的Markdown或JSON格式，以便于在智能工作流程中使用。对于Yves来说，这个工具可以帮助Logic Labs更高效地处理和分析文档数据，从而支持AI项目的开发和创新，也为TexTale提供更加简便的数据管理，提升电商运营的整体效率。作为创意设计从业者，使用这个工具可以更方便地整理和利用文献资料，加速创意过程。

github_trending.description: 张雪峰.skill — 张雪峰的认知操作系统。高考志愿/考研/职业规划的实战思维框架。由女娲.skill生成。
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个针对高考志愿、考研和职业规划的实用思维框架，旨在帮助用户更好地进行人生规划和决策。对于Yves来说，这个项目可以为Logic Labs的AI产品开发提供实用的思维模型，同时在TexTale的品牌营销中也能借鉴其系统化思考的方式，提升创意设计的效率和精准度。

github_trending.description: A self-hosted travel/trip planner with real-time collaboration, interactive maps, PWA support, SSO, budgets, packing lists, and more.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个自托管的旅行计划工具，允许用户实时协作、查看互动地图、管理预算和打包清单等功能。对Yves的TexTale（科技服装电商）业务特别有帮助，因为它可以帮助规划与品牌活动或市场推广相关的旅行，同时也能提升团体旅行的效率和体验。作为创意设计从业者，这个工具可激发更多创意，优化旅行项目的策划与执行。

github_trending.description: AI 时代的伯克希尔：基于 Claude Code 的价值投资研究框架。巴菲特·芒格·段永平·李录四大师方法论 + 多Agent并行研究。| AI-era Berkshire: a value investing research framework built on Claude Code. 4 masters' methodologies + multi-agent adversarial analysis.
github_trending.summary_zh: 这个项目是一个研究框架，旨在利用 AI 技术进行价值投资的分析，结合了巴菲特、芒格等投资大师的投资方法。对于Yves来说，这个项目对Logic Labs（AI 公司）的业务有帮助，因为它能够为AI技术的应用提供新的视角和工具，同时提升其在金融领域的创造性应用能力。
